EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front caused flooding and wind damage over portions of the islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Kaneohe had approximately 4,990 customers without power. Haleiwa had approximately 1,510 customers without power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front caused flooding and wind damage over portions of the islands.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A plume of deep tropical moisture moved over the Hawaiian islands from east to west in association with a surface trough. This moisture combined with an upper level trough led to heavy rainfall, isolated thunderstorms, and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple Roads within Hilo and Mountainview were closed or partially closed due to flooding. Additionally, there was a vehicle submerged underwater on South Kopua Road in Punas Eden Roc neighborhood near Mountain View. The roads closed included Pohaku Dr, Enos Road, Mauna Loa Road, N Kulani Rd, Kahikopele St, Pohaku St between 39th Ave and 40th Av, Kamehameha Ave, Pauahi St, E Kawailani Rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall over portions of southwest Kauai led to extreme drought conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The D3 drought improved through the week as a result of increased rainfall and streamflow supported improvements over the northeastern and eastern areas of the island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brush fire in Kahili broke out and burned four to five acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A brush fire near Kahili Golf Course closed Honoapiilani and Kuihelani Highways around 12:30 p.m. on Saturday, Nov. 16, 2024, due to smoke and reduced visibility. Northbound traffic was closed between Honoapiilani and Kuihelani while southbound traffic was being turned around at Maui Tropical Plantation.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall led to severe to extreme drought conditions over the western portions of Molokai.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: During the week of November 5th through November 12th, the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) and condition reports supported going from a D2 to a D3 extreme drought on the southwest side of Molokai.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough destabilized the atmosphere and led to heavy rain that caused isolated flooding impacts on the Big Island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Kuakini Highway was closed between Onioni St and Queen Kaahumanu Hwy due to flooding.

A strong low pressure system to the northwest of the Hawaiian Islands along with an associated surface trough brought abundant moisture and an unstable atmosphere to the region. This moisture and instability led to heavy rainfall and thunderstorms primarily over the island of Kauai. During the early morning of April twelve, a band of heavy showers with embedded thunderstorms moved over the island of Kauai. There were several evacuations, road closures, and flooding reports that resulted over portions of Kauai.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Kuhio Highway was closed in both directions at the Hanalei Bridge.

Two of the people were pulled out of the water unconscious and one person was pulled out of the water conscious by Ocean Safety. One person was pronounced dead at the scene by Honolulu Emergency Medical Services and another was taken to the hospital. Another person was taken to the hospital in serious condition.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Hone, which formed in the Central Pacific, tracked towards the Hawaiian islands and passed by just south of the Big Island. Hurricane Hone's outer rain bands brought flooding to portions of the Big Island and the close proximity of the center of the storm brought widespread tropical storm force winds with pockets of power outages over the Big Island and Maui. Thousands of customers reportedly lost power and utility poles were damaged.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: As quoted in the Star-Advertiser, the Hawaii County Civil Defense Administrator reported the county had received thirty eight damage claims from Kohala to Kau and that, out of the thirty eight damage reports, eighteen had been verified. Nine of these damage reports were major, meaning they may be uninhabitable. One of these properties included a home used as a bed and breakfast experienced extensive flooding with massive mud, boulder, and debris buildup in the home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Hone, which formed in the Central Pacific, tracked towards the Hawaiian islands and passed by just south of the Big Island. Hurricane Hone's outer rain bands brought flooding to portions of the Big Island and the close proximity of the center of the storm brought widespread tropical storm force winds with pockets of power outages over the Big Island and Maui. Thousands of customers reportedly lost power and utility poles were damaged.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from the outer bands of Hurricane Hone caused flooding which closed several roads over the southeast portion of the Big Island.  In the Kau District, Highway 11 was closed at Kawa Flats. Wood Valley Road and Cane Haul Road were closed near Pahala. In the Puna District, North Kulani Road was closed. Many of the roads were damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall over the west-southwest portions of Maui led to very dry extreme drought conditions.
Below average rainfall over the west-southwest portions of Maui led to very dry extreme drought conditions.
Below average rainfall over the west-southwest portions of Maui led to very dry extreme drought conditions.

Below average December rainfall totals at most locations across the main Hawaiian Islands exacerbated statewide drought conditions. Extreme drought (D3) developed over leeward Oahu and leeward Maui. D3 drought conditions remained over the leeward areas of Kauai and Molokai. Approximately 3% of the state was under D3 drought at the end of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reports from the USDA-FSA and the State Department of Agriculture stated that the agricultural impacts of the extreme (D3) drought conditions included very dry conditions over the rangelands of leeward Maui County including Molokai through the month. A rancher operating in the leeward side of Haleakala on the island of Maui reported having to reduce his herd size by 50 percent as there was insufficient pasture forage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers along a dissipating cold front spawned a funnel cloud near Kauai.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An employee at the NWS Data Collection Office at Lihue Airport and an observer at the FAA tower reported a funnel cloud.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong surface high pressure system to the northeast of the Hawaiian Islands caused strong east-northeasterly trade winds to blow across the state.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The area in front of 3300 Tantalus Drive was closed in both directions due to multiple fallen trees.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A snorkeler drowned at Three Tables Beach as a result of rising swell and surf.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A drowning occurred at Shark's Cove as 18 to 20 foot surf rolled in. A High Surf Warning was in effect for Oahu's North Shore.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A drowning occurred at Shark's Cove on the North Shore of Oahu as 18 to 20 foot surf rolled in. A High Surf Warning was in effect.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average December rainfall totals at most locations across the main Hawaiian Islands exacerbated statewide drought conditions. Extreme drought (D3) developed over leeward Oahu and leeward Maui. D3 drought conditions remained over the leeward areas of Kauai and Molokai. Approximately 3% of the state was under D3 drought at the end of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reports from the USDA-FSA and the State Department of Agriculture stated that the agricultural impacts of the extreme (D3) drought conditions included very dry conditions over the rangelands of leeward Kauai through the month. The USDA on Kauai reported that most of the ranchers on the island have been impacted by drought. Ranchers have been providing supplemental feed and have been hauling water for their cattle.
